# R.A.H. Solutions, LLC — Full Reference

Landscaping company in Edgerton, WI specializing in landscape design, lawn care, hardscaping, concrete services, and year-round property maintenance.

## Business Information
- Owner: Robert Harried
- Phone: (608) 501-5123

- Address: 262 County Road W, Edgerton, WI 53534
- Hours: Mon–Fri 7:00am–5:00pm, Saturday 7:00am–12:00pm, Sunday Closed
- Service Area: Edgerton, Stoughton, Janesville, Madison, WI — Rock and Dane counties, 50-mile radius
- Licensed: Yes, fully licensed and insured in Wisconsin
- Insured: Yes
- Founded: 2023
- Business Type: Family-owned and operated, locally rooted in Edgerton, WI

## Full About
R.A.H. Solutions, LLC is a family-owned landscaping and outdoor improvement company founded in 2023 by Robert Harried in Edgerton, Wisconsin. Located at 262 County Road W, the company serves homeowners and small businesses throughout Edgerton, Stoughton, Janesville, and Madison — covering most of Rock County and southern Dane County within a 50-mile radius. R.A.H. Solutions takes a comprehensive approach to outdoor spaces, handling everything from initial landscape design and full installation through ongoing maintenance, hardscaping, concrete work, and winter snow removal. The company is fully licensed and insured and operates with a personalized service model, using the same crew members across repeat visits and tailoring programs to each property's specific soil, climate, and aesthetic needs.

## Service Details

### Lawn Maintenance
Scheduled lawn maintenance programs including mowing, edging, trimming, and blowing. Programs are customized by property size and frequency — weekly, bi-weekly, or seasonal. Typical cost: $40–$80 per visit for residential properties. Serving Edgerton, WI and surrounding Rock and Dane County communities.

### Landscape Design
Custom landscape design services for residential and commercial properties. Includes site consultation, plant selection appropriate for Wisconsin's climate and soil, grading considerations, and phased implementation planning. Design services are coordinated with installation for a seamless experience.

### Landscape Installation
Full-service landscape installation bringing designs to life — planting trees, shrubs, perennials, installing edging, mulch, sod, and hardscape elements. R.A.H. Solutions handles complete yard transformations from bare ground to finished landscape. Timeline: most residential installs complete in 1–5 days depending on scope.

### Hardscaping Services
Design and installation of patios, retaining walls, walkways, steps, and outdoor living spaces using natural stone, pavers, and concrete block. All hardscape installations include proper base preparation (6–8 inches of compacted gravel minimum) to prevent frost heave during Wisconsin's freeze-thaw cycles. Properly installed hardscapes last 25–30 years.

### Concrete Services
Professional concrete installation for driveways, patios, walkways, and steps. Uses air-entrained concrete mixes rated for Wisconsin winters. All concrete is placed on properly compacted aggregate base (6 inches minimum) to prevent settling and cracking. Residential driveways: 4–6 inches thick. Finishes available: broom, exposed aggregate, stamped.

### Tree Pruning
Professional tree pruning including crown thinning, canopy lifting, deadwood removal, and storm damage limb cleanup. Pruning is timed to Wisconsin seasons — most deciduous trees in late winter before bud break. Promotes long-term health, reduces wind resistance, and maintains clearances from structures.

### Shrub Trimming
Expert shaping and pruning of ornamental shrubs, hedges, and foundation plantings. Proper pruning technique preserves natural form while maintaining health. Formal hedges shaped for geometric appearance; naturalistic plantings shaped to enhance their growth habit.

### Garden Maintenance
Ongoing garden bed care including weeding, deadheading, pruning, seasonal planting, and mulch replenishment. Monthly or seasonal programs available. Keeps beds looking polished while protecting soil health throughout Wisconsin's growing season.

### Mulching Services
Professional mulch installation for landscape beds, trees, and gardens. Benefits include moisture retention, weed suppression, temperature regulation for plant roots, and improved curb appeal. Typical application: 2–3 inches deep. Replenishment recommended annually.

### Sod Installation
Instant lawn installation using fresh-cut sod — Kentucky bluegrass, tall fescue, and fescue blends suited to Wisconsin. Includes site preparation, grading, soil amendment if needed, and a 30-day watering plan. Sod takes root and establishes in 2–6 weeks under proper care.

### Lawn Restoration
Complete renovation for bare, thinning, compacted, or grub-damaged lawns. Services include core aeration, overseeding, thatch removal, topdressing with compost, and soil amendment. Best performed in fall (ideal) or early spring for Wisconsin cool-season grasses. Noticeable improvement within one growing season.

### Spring Yard Cleanup
Comprehensive spring cleanup to prepare the property for the growing season. Includes debris removal, leaf cleanup (if not completed in fall), bed edging, first mowing, and bed preparation for spring planting. Most clients schedule in April–May after last frost risk has passed.

### Fall Yard Cleanup
Professional fall cleanup including leaf removal, bed cleanup and cutback, final mowing, and winterization. Leaves are blown, raked, and removed or mulched back into the lawn. Beds are cleaned to reduce pest habitat and disease overwintering. Typically scheduled October–November before first hard freeze.

### Snow Removal
Reliable residential and commercial snow removal throughout Edgerton and surrounding areas. Services include plowing driveways and parking lots, shoveling walkways and entry points, and de-icing. Seasonal contracts and per-event pricing available. Response triggered at 2-inch accumulation threshold by default. Same-day service on most storms.

### Excavating Services
Professional excavation and earthmoving for landscaping, drainage, and site preparation projects. Services include site grading, swale and drainage solution installation, pond and water feature excavation, and utility trench digging. Equipped with compact and full-size excavation equipment appropriate for residential and commercial properties.

### Residential Lawn Care
Comprehensive lawn care programs built for homeowners. Includes recurring mowing, edging, trimming, blowing, and seasonal applications. Same crew every visit for consistency. Programs customized by lot size, turf type, and desired service frequency. Serving Edgerton neighborhoods and surrounding Rock County communities.

### Commercial Lawn Care
Scheduled grounds maintenance for businesses, commercial properties, HOAs, and multi-family properties. Reliable, contract-based service with flexible scheduling to meet business needs. Includes mowing, trimming, edging, seasonal cleanup, and snow removal. Properties in Edgerton, Janesville, Stoughton, and Madison areas.

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What landscaping services does R.A.H. Solutions offer in Edgerton, WI?
A: R.A.H. Solutions provides 17 professional services including landscape design, hardscape installation (patios, retaining walls, walkways), lawn maintenance, concrete services, tree pruning, shrub trimming, mulching, sod installation, spring and fall yard cleanup, and year-round snow removal for residential and commercial properties in Edgerton, WI.

Q: What areas of Southern Wisconsin does R.A.H. Solutions serve?
A: Our primary service area is Edgerton, WI. We regularly serve Stoughton, Janesville, and Madison, covering most of Rock and Dane counties within a 50-mile radius of our Edgerton location at 262 County Road W.

Q: How much does a landscaping project typically cost in Edgerton?
A: Costs vary by scope. Lawn maintenance programs start around $40–$80 per visit. Full landscape installations range from $2,000 to $20,000 or more depending on size and complexity. Hardscape patios typically run $3,000–$15,000. Concrete driveways average $4,000–$10,000. R.A.H. Solutions provides free on-site estimates with no obligation.

Q: How often should I mow my lawn in Wisconsin?
A: During the active growing season (May through September), most Edgerton lawns need mowing every 7–10 days. Cool-season grasses like Kentucky bluegrass and fescue grow fastest in spring and fall, sometimes requiring weekly cuts. We adjust frequency based on your lawn and the season.

Q: Do you offer seasonal snow removal contracts in Edgerton?
A: Yes. We offer seasonal contracts (fixed monthly rate regardless of storms) and per-event pricing. Seasonal contracts provide predictable budgeting for homeowners and commercial properties. Service is triggered at 2-inch accumulation by default, with same-day response on most storms.

Q: Are you licensed and insured to work in Wisconsin?
A: Yes, R.A.H. Solutions is fully licensed and insured to perform landscaping, concrete, and excavation work in Wisconsin. We maintain all required coverage to protect clients, their properties, and our crew on every project.

Q: How soon can you start a landscaping project?
A: Scheduling depends on season and workload. Spring cleanups and urgent requests are accommodated as quickly as possible. Larger installations are typically scheduled 1–3 weeks out. Call (608) 501-5123 to discuss your timeline and we'll find the earliest availability.

Q: Can you work with an existing landscape or limited budget?
A: Absolutely. We work with clients at all budget levels and can phase improvements over multiple seasons. Our team helps prioritize work that delivers the most visual impact and functionality first, then plans follow-up phases to match your timeline.

Q: How do I prevent concrete from cracking in Wisconsin winters?
A: Key factors are proper base prep (6-inch compacted aggregate), air-entrained concrete mix rated for freeze-thaw cycles, correct slab thickness (4–6 inches minimum), proper curing, and control joints placed at appropriate intervals. R.A.H. Solutions follows these standards on every pour.

Q: What's the best time to install sod in Wisconsin?
A: Late spring (May–June) or early fall (August–September) are ideal. Fall is often preferred because cooler temperatures reduce stress on new sod and moisture requirements are lower. Sod installed in summer heat requires intensive daily watering for 2–3 weeks to establish successfully.
